prompt: Four women in national dress — German dirndl, British tweed, American denim, Canadian plaid — each standing on a pile of era-specific objects, all reaching the same height. But as each woman rises, her body subtly warps and bends — the space around her upper body visually distorts, compresses, curves inward like a gravitational lens. Not a ceiling. Not a wall. Just reality itself resisting their ascent. The distortion is identical for all four women at the same height. Below: warm amber light, solid and real. Above: reality bends. Melancholic WPA mural oil painting, single frame. No text. No signs. No labels. No charts.
concept: Four women from different countries standing on piles of accumulated labor from their respective histories, all reaching the exact same height — visualizing how different national trajectories converged at the same participation rate.
metaphor: Space warps and compresses around each woman as she rises making invisible systemic resistance visible without a literal ceiling.
